2009-01-01.title: Police Patrols, With Smiles That Never Tire
2009-01-01.abstract: In a country that calls itself the Land of Smiles, highway policemen will spend the first week of the year wearing anti-pollution masks painted with smiles to lift motorists’ moods.

2009-01-01.title: Green Zone, Heart of U.S. Occupation, Reverts to Iraqi Control
2009-01-01.abstract: For nearly six years, the Green Zone has become such an enduring shorthand for a willfully sequestered occupation of Iraq that it is easy to forget it is an actual place.

2009-01-01.title: An Egyptian Border Town’s Commerce, Conducted via Tunnels, Comes to a Halt
2009-01-01.abstract: Israel’s continuing air assaults on Hamas sites are also disrupting the livelihoods of the people of Rafah, the smuggling of goods through a network of tunnels.

2009-01-01.title: Attacks Continue in Gaza Conflict
2009-01-01.abstract: With air attacks on Gaza in their fifth day, Israel said it would keep pressure on Hamas but was open to future diplomacy.

2009-01-01.title: Striking Deep Into Israel, Hamas Employs an Upgraded Arsenal
2009-01-01.abstract: Hamas’s arsenal has been upgraded with weapons parts smuggled into Gaza since it seized control of the territory 18 months ago, according to U.S. and Israeli officials.

2009-01-02.title: Clinton, Familiar With Pitfalls of Mideast Politics, May Face Early Test in Gaza
2009-01-02.abstract: Hillary Clinton’s troubled history with Arab-Israeli issues could complicate her task of proving that she can be a mediator in the Middle East.

2009-01-02.title: Thailand: Victims of Nightclub Fire Include a Foreigner
2009-01-02.abstract: The Bangkok police said Thursday that one foreigner, a Singaporean, died in a fire at a high-end Bangkok nightclub on New Year’s Eve that killed at least 59 people.

2009-01-02.title: The Vatican: Pope Urges ‘Profound’ Change of Vision
2009-01-02.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI said on Thursday that the world was in “a cultural and moral crisis” and that the global financial crisis merited a “profound” change of vision.

2009-01-02.title: Corrections
2009-01-02.abstract: An article on Wednesday about Egypt’s response to the Israeli assault on Gaza described their geographical relationship incorrectly. Gaza abuts northern Egypt, not southern Egypt.

2009-01-02.title: Podcast: Green Zone Reverts to Iraqi Control
2009-01-02.abstract: Campbell Robertson attended the handover ceremonies on Thursday that marked the passing of the headquarters of the American occupation of Iraq from U.S. to Iraq control.

2009-01-03.title: Egypt Pressed on Gaza From Without and Within
2009-01-03.abstract: A sustained Israeli operation in Gaza would sharply raise public pressure to do more to help the Palestinians there.

2009-01-03.title: Taking a Break From War With a Game Anything but Gentle
2009-01-03.abstract: In Pushtesurkh, Afghan men play buzkashi, a sport that tests horse-riding skills and the strength of a goat carcass.

2009-01-03.title: A Chinese Chef’s Long, Diverted Path to 3 Stars
2009-01-03.abstract: Chan Yan-tak, the first Chinese chef to earn Michelin’s top ranking, quit the industry to be a stay-at-home dad but was coaxed back.

2009-01-03.title: Russia: Ukraine Accused of Seizing Fuel
2009-01-03.abstract: Gazprom, the Russian gas monopoly, accused Ukraine on Friday of diverting fuel intended for Europe and using it within Ukraine.

2009-01-03.title: Canada: Charge in Afghan Death
2009-01-03.abstract: The Canadian military police charged an officer with second-degree murder on Friday in connection with the shooting death of an unarmed man in Afghanistan.

2009-01-04.title: The Last Village
2009-01-04.abstract: The residents of Kampong Buangkok, the last traditional village in Singapore discuss how much they are going to miss this   way of life now that the goverment plans to take over the land.

2009-01-04.title: Singapore Prepares to Gobble Up Its Last Village
2009-01-04.abstract: The demolition of Singapore’s last rural village is a final step in an extreme national makeover.

2009-01-04.title: Correction
2009-01-04.abstract: An article last Sunday rendered a comment by Israeli foreign minister, Tzipi Livni, incorrectly. On NBC’s “Meet the Press,” she said, “We are targeting Hamas, we are not looking for civilians to kill,” and then added “More than that, during this military operation, we are trying to avoid any kind of civilian casualties.” She did not say, “We are targeting Hamas, we are not looking for civilians to kill more than that.”

2009-01-04.title: Correction
2009-01-04.abstract: Because of an editing error, an article last Sunday about the decision by a Chinese dairy association to financially compensate the families whose children were made ill by tainted milk misstated a quality of the chemical melamine. When added to milk, melamine only appears to boost the milk’s protein content; it does not actually do so.

2009-01-04.title: Oliver Lincoln Lundquist, Designer, Is Dead at 92
2009-01-04.abstract: Mr. Lundquist, an architect and industrial designer, led the team that created the United Nations logo.

2009-01-05.title: Europe Sends Two Missions to Promote a Cease-Fire
2009-01-05.abstract: Seeking to fill a diplomatic vacuum in the Middle East left by the departing Bush administration, the missions will try to broker a cease-fire in Gaza and offer more humanitarian aid.

2009-01-05.title: In Eastern Europe, Lives Languish in Mental Facilities
2009-01-05.abstract: Many people with mental illnesses or disabilities are sequestered without rights or recourse under Communist-era rules that put their fates in the hands of legal guardians.

2009-01-05.title: Hezbollah Answers Israel With Speeches
2009-01-05.abstract: Despite talk in recent days about the possible opening of a second front on the Lebanese border, it is unlikely that Hezbollah will attack unless Hamas’s situation becomes desperate, analysts say.

2009-01-05.title: Kidnappings in Mexico Send Shivers Across Border
2009-01-05.abstract: A string of kidnappings targeting people with family in the United States has caused many people to flee Mexico.

2009-01-05.title: Gaza Hospital Fills Up, Mainly With Civilians
2009-01-05.abstract: Doctors have been battling to keep Shifa Hospital in Gaza City running under the most adverse circumstances, but scant resources are being stretched to a breaking point.

2009-01-06.title: Belgian Market’s Luster Dims, but Legacy Stays
2009-01-06.abstract: Antwerp’s Orthodox Jews have set the tone of its lively diamond market for more than a century, but global economic forces threaten their livelihoods and way of life.

2009-01-06.title: Israel and Gaza Conflict Continues
2009-01-06.abstract: On the third day of the ground offensive in Gaza, civilians continue to get caught up in the bombardments.

2009-01-06.title: Warnings Not Enough for Gaza Families
2009-01-06.abstract: Gaza’s civilians — particularly those in militant strongholds — are finding it difficult to evacuate their homes before Israeli attacks begin.

2009-01-06.title: In Fatah-Governed West Bank, Solidarity With Hamas
2009-01-06.abstract: The Fatah-led Palestinian Authority is deeply worried about popular support for rival Hamas over the conflict in Gaza, to the point of crushing demonstrations.

2009-01-06.title: India Seeks Extraditions in Mumbai Siege
2009-01-06.abstract: India produced what it said was the first comprehensive evidence linking the Mumbai attackers to Pakistan.

2009-01-07.title: Atheists Send a Message, on 800 British Buses
2009-01-07.abstract: An ad campaign for atheism in London is aiming to counter religious ads, one bus billboard at a time.

2009-01-07.title: For Israel, 2006 Lessons but Old Pitfalls
2009-01-07.abstract: Israel is applying the military insight it gained from the 2006 war against Hezbollah in Lebanon, but diplomatic lessons do not seem to have been so well applied.

2009-01-07.title: This Time, Iraqis Hear and See Candidates
2009-01-07.abstract: The nation’s provincial elections on Jan. 31 are the first in which the government has deemed it safe enough for the names of office seekers to appear on the ballot.

2009-01-07.title: Azerbaijan Bars Foreigners From Use of Its FM Band
2009-01-07.abstract: The move to enforce a law that bans foreign companies from broadcasting in the country effectively bans Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty, Voice of America and the BBC.

2009-01-07.title: Guards Plead Not Guilty in ’07 Killings in Baghdad
2009-01-07.abstract: The five guards for Blackwater International pleaded not guilty in federal court to manslaughter in the case of the 2007 shootings in Baghdad that left at least 17 Iraqis dead.

2009-01-08.title: Israelis Honor Fallen Soldiers, While Seeing the Gaza Campaign as Justified
2009-01-08.abstract: Israelis are not relishing what lies ahead if the offensive on Gaza continues, but the majority remains staunchly behind the operation.

2009-01-08.title: Gaza War Role Is Political Lift for Ex-Premier
2009-01-08.abstract: The popularity of Israel’s Defense Minister Ehud Barak is soaring, yet he was not eager to strike Hamas.

2009-01-08.title: Gunman in Mumbai Siege a Pakistani, Official Says
2009-01-08.abstract: Pakistan’s national security adviser, Mahmud Ali Durrani, was fired hours after confirming that the lone surviving gunman from the Mumbai attacks is a Pakistani citizen.

2009-01-08.title: Tight Security Across Iraq on a Holy Day for Shiites
2009-01-08.abstract: Millions of Iraqi Shiites celebrated one of the most emotional occasions in their religious calendar Wednesday, accompanied by stringent security measures.

2009-01-08.title: In Crime Wave, an Interrupted Meal Haunts Mexico
2009-01-08.abstract: Felix Batista, an American security consultant who specialized in resolving kidnappings, was himself abducted in Mexico while eating at a restaurant.

2009-01-09.title: For a Lone Mechanic, Cuba Is Still Hog Heaven
2009-01-09.abstract: With spare and homemade parts, Sergio Morales grants new life to Cuba’s few remaining Harley-Davidson motorcycles.

2009-01-09.title: Arrests of Officers in Turkey Stoke Uneasiness in Military
2009-01-09.abstract: Three retired generals, along with more than 30 other suspects, were arrested as part of an investigation into a possible coup plot.

2009-01-09.title: 2 Qaeda Leaders Killed in U.S. Strike in Pakistan
2009-01-09.abstract: An American missile strike on Jan. 1 in Pakistan killed two leaders of Al Qaeda, an American official said.

2009-01-09.title: For Redress of Grievances, Mexicans Turn to Bureaucracy Contest
2009-01-09.abstract: A woman who had to endure a monthly bureaucratic odyssey to get medication for her son won a government-sponsored contest to identify Mexico’s most useless red tape.

2009-01-09.title: Corrections
2009-01-09.abstract: Because of an editing error, an article on Dec. 24 about the resignation of the speaker of the Iraqi Parliament, Mahmoud al-Mashhadani, misidentified his political party. It is the National Dialogue Council, not the Iraqi Islamic Party.

2009-01-10.title: Ghana’s Unlikely Democrat Finds Vindication in Vote
2009-01-10.abstract: Jerry John Rawlings was an unlikely savior for a country that represented Africa’s brightest hopes, but that in its disappointment became emblematic of a continent’s malaise.

2009-01-10.title: For Arab Clan, Days of Agony in a Cross-Fire
2009-01-10.abstract: Few families have suffered the wrath of the Israeli military as calamitously as the Samounis.

2009-01-10.title: As Biden Meets Pakistani Leaders, Bombs Rattle a Cultural Hub
2009-01-10.abstract: Five small bombs exploded outside theaters in Lahore, hours after Joseph R. Biden Jr. arrived in Islamabad to meet Pakistan’s military and civilian leaders.

2009-01-10.title: Global Financial Crisis Upends the Plans of Many South Koreans to Study Abroad
2009-01-10.abstract: The global financial crisis has decimated the value of South Korea’s currency, forcing tens of thousands of students to alter their study plans, or cancel them altogether.

2009-01-10.title: Costa Rica: Quake Kills at Least 15
2009-01-10.abstract: At least 15 people were killed and 42 were still missing on Friday after an earthquake set off landslides in the mountains of northern Costa Rica.

2009-01-11.title: As Troops Enter Gaza City, Israel Sees an Opening
2009-01-11.abstract: Senior Israeli officials said they believed that the Hamas military wing was beginning to crack and that Hamas leaders inside Gaza were looking for a cease-fire.

2009-01-11.title: 200 People Missing as Ferry Sinks in Indonesia
2009-01-11.abstract: More than 200 people were missing and feared dead after a passenger ferry sank in high seas off the coast of the Indonesian island of Sulawesi.

2009-01-11.title: 46 Die in Taliban Attack on Pakistani Troops
2009-01-11.abstract: A violent weekend was a new sign of the lawlessness of the tribal areas along Pakistan’s western border with Afghanistan.

2009-01-11.title: Iraqi Lawmakers Delay Naming a New Speaker
2009-01-11.abstract: The Iraqi Parliament delayed the selection of a new speaker on Sunday as Sunni lawmakers squabbled over which Sunni faction should get the post.

2009-01-11.title: In Interview, Obama Talks of ‘New Approach’ to Iran
2009-01-11.abstract: Barack Obama confirmed that he intended to pursue a policy of engagement with Iran and said he wanted “a new emphasis on respect.”

2009-01-12.title: Without Gas, Bulgarians Turn Icy to Old Ally
2009-01-12.abstract: Many in Bulgaria accused Russia of instigating a new cold war, depriving millions of Europeans of heat and fuel to strike a political blow against the West.

2009-01-12.title: 2 Britons Die During Peak Ascent in the Alps
2009-01-12.abstract: Trying an ascent of a peak in the Mont Blanc mountain range, Rob Gauntlett, the youngest Briton to reach the summit of Mount Everest, fell to his death with another Briton, James Atkinson.

2009-01-12.title: Gas Deal in Europe Is Undone and Redone
2009-01-12.abstract: A deal between Russia and Ukraine to restore heating fuel seemed to be falling apart, with Moscow objecting to conditions that Ukraine attached to the agreement after Russia had signed it.

2009-01-12.title: Crisis Imperils 2-State Plan, Shifting a Balance
2009-01-12.abstract: Officials in Egypt and Jordan fear they will be pressed to absorb Palestinian populations beyond their borders.

2009-01-12.title: U.N. Warns of Refugee Crisis in Gaza Strip
2009-01-12.abstract: According to the United Nations, about 30,000 Gazans are living in schools it sponsors and an estimated 60,000 have fled to the houses of relatives.

2009-01-13.title: Indonesia: 250 Feared Dead in Aftermath of Ferry Accident
2009-01-13.abstract: Rescuers were losing hope Monday of finding about 250 people still lost at sea after a ferry capsized off the coast of the Indonesian island of Sulawesi.

2009-01-13.title: Germany: Economic Stimulus
2009-01-13.abstract: Chancellor Angela Merkel, who had resisted pressure for a quick and large stimulus plan, reversed course under pressure from her critics in Germany and abroad.

2009-01-13.title: Pio Laghi, Papal Envoy, Dies at 86
2009-01-13.abstract: Cardinal Laghi served as the Vatican’s first papal nuncio to Washington until 1990, and in 2003 urged President Bush not to start the war in Iraq.

2009-01-13.title: Top Officer Urges Limit on Mission of Military
2009-01-13.abstract: Adm. Mike Mullen, chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, said Monday that senior officers must work to prevent the militarization of American foreign policy.

2009-01-13.title: Gulf Oil States Seeking a Lead in Clean Energy
2009-01-13.abstract: Well aware of the limits of oil, the United Arab Emirates, Qatar and Saudi Arabia are aggressively pouring billions of dollars into green technologies.

2009-01-14.title: Critic of Chechen President Is Killed in Exile in Vienna
2009-01-14.abstract: A Chechen who had formally accused the president of Chechnya of participating in torture sessions was fatally shot as he walked out of a grocery store in Vienna, according to his lawyer.

2009-01-14.title: Spain: 14 Salvadorans to Face Trial
2009-01-14.abstract: A judge on the High Court said that he would prosecute 14 former members of the Salvadoran Army accused in the 1989 killings of six Jesuit priests, their housekeeper and her daughter.

2009-01-14.title: Iran Says U.S. Helped Finance Overthrow Plot
2009-01-14.abstract: Iran has tried four Iranians on charges of trying to overthrow the government with the support of the United States, a judiciary spokesman said Tuesday.

2009-01-14.title: Clinton Pledges Tough Diplomacy and a Fast Start
2009-01-14.abstract: Hillary Rodham Clinton parried doubts about her husband’s fund-raising and emerged from a hearing headed for swift approval as secretary of state.

2009-01-14.title: Israel Halts Ship to Gaza, Iran Says
2009-01-14.abstract: The Israeli Navy intercepted an Iranian ship carrying humanitarian aid for people in Gaza, Iran’s state-run radio reported Tuesday.

2009-01-15.title: Chávez Lets West Make Oil Bids as Prices Plunge
2009-01-15.abstract: The financial crisis is hurting Venezuelan President Hugo Chávez’s efforts to establish a Socialist-inspired state.

2009-01-15.title: Military Planners, in Nod to Obama, Are Preparing for a Faster Iraq Withdrawal
2009-01-15.abstract: The new plans would provide alternatives to a timetable drawn up by the top commanders to bring troops home more slowly than Barack Obama promised during his campaign.

2009-01-15.title: Chinese Officials Gamble, and Their Luck Runs Out
2009-01-15.abstract: Communist officials have pillaged state funds to try their luck at casinos in Macao, government prosecutors say.

2009-01-15.title: Chinese Chemical Plant Site Moves After Outcry
2009-01-15.abstract: The Chinese Environmental Ministry has approved a petrochemical plant that drew fierce opposition over feared pollution. But the approval is based on the plant’s being built in another city.

2009-01-15.title: Suspect Is Held in Slaying of Chechen Exile
2009-01-15.abstract: Austrian police held a suspect in the public slaying of a Chechen exile who had accused Chechnya’s president of ordering and participating in kidnappings and torture.

2009-01-16.title: Fighting Continues in Gaza
2009-01-16.abstract: Ethan Bronner, Jerusalem bureau chief, reports from northwest Gaza, where he is embedded with the Israeli Army.

2009-01-16.title: Opponents of Mugabe Tell Court of Torture
2009-01-16.abstract: More than a dozen activists say they were tortured to obtain false confessions after they were abducted by agents of President Robert Mugabe’s government.

2009-01-16.title: Art Installation Stirs Europe's Ire
2009-01-16.abstract: A mosaic installed in the European Council in Brussels has angered some by depicting stereotypes of member countries.

2009-01-16.title: Corrections
2009-01-16.abstract: The Memo From Moscow article on Wednesday, about the underlying issues that have complicated the natural-gas dispute between Russia and Ukraine, misstated the portion of the Ukrainian population that is ethnic Russian. It is about 17 percent, not “nearly half.”.

2009-01-16.title: Corrections
2009-01-16.abstract: An article last Friday about American military plans in Afghanistan and personnel changes at the Pentagon misstated the year that Michele A. Flournoy, chosen as under secretary of Defense for policy by President-elect Barack Obama, was the principal writer for a Quadrennial Defense Review issued by the Clinton administration. It was 1997, not 2001.

2009-01-17.title: Baltic Riots Spread to Lithuania in the Face of Deteriorating Economic Conditions
2009-01-17.abstract: A group of 7,000 gathered in Vilnius, the capital, to protest planned economic austerity measures, including pay cuts, falling pensions and rising taxes.

2009-01-17.title: Older Son of bin Laden Believed to Be in Pakistan
2009-01-17.abstract: After years of house arrest in Iran, Saad bin Laden is likely operating in Pakistan, a senior U.S. official said.

2009-01-17.title: New U.S. Envoy to Russia Echoes Father Who Fought for Soviets
2009-01-17.abstract: John Beyrle, the new American ambassador to Russia, grew up listening to war stories from his father, who fought with the Soviets during World War II.

2009-01-17.title: Gunmen Kill Iraqi Cleric Campaigning for Council
2009-01-17.abstract: Haitham Kadhim al-Husaini, a member of Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki’s political party, was killed as he drove away from a campaign event in a province south of Baghdad.

2009-01-17.title: The Many Sources Governing Warfare
2009-01-17.abstract: International law governing warfare is based on the four Geneva conventions, which were revised and expanded after World War II.

2009-01-18.title: In Homes and on Streets, a War That Feels Deadlier
2009-01-18.abstract: For residents of the southern Gaza town of Rafah, this war, they say, feels deadlier than previous ones.

2009-01-18.title: Tensions Rise on Korean Peninsula
2009-01-18.abstract: South Korea placed more soldiers at frontier guard posts, a day after the North Korean military declared an “all-out confrontational posture” against the South.

2009-01-18.title: Rebuilding Begins Upon a Wobbly Truce
2009-01-18.abstract: European leaders gathered in Jerusalem as Israel sought help in converting a fragile pause in the Gaza fighting into a blueprint for a more durable calm.

2009-01-18.title: Gazan Doctor Loses Family
2009-01-18.abstract: Dr. Izzeldin Abuelaish has devoted his life to reconciliation between Israelis and Palestinians. Now he is struggling to hold on to the humane philosophy that has guided his life.

2009-01-18.title: Russia and Ukraine Reach Deal on Gas
2009-01-18.abstract: The prime ministers of Russia and Ukraine agreed to a resolution of their countries’ gas dispute early Sunday.

2009-01-19.title: Another Politician Is Killed as Iraqi Voting Draws Near
2009-01-19.abstract: A prominent Sunni tribal sheik running in the coming provincial elections in northern Iraq was killed Sunday by a suicide bomber.

2009-01-19.title: Finding Treasures in a City’s Disappearing Past
2009-01-19.abstract: Returning to Beijing after the Cultural Revolution, Li Songtang devoted himself to rescuing scraps of history, work made more urgent by today’s redevelopment.

2009-01-19.title: Shocked and Grieving Gazans Find Bodies Under the Rubble of Homes
2009-01-19.abstract: As the people of Gaza emerged from hiding on Sunday, they confronted the full, sometimes breathtaking extent of the destruction.

2009-01-19.title: Parsing Gains of Gaza War
2009-01-19.abstract: Have three weeks of overpowering war by Israel weakened Hamas as Israel had hoped, or simply caused acute human suffering?

2009-01-19.title: Ex-Senator Considered as Envoy to Mideast
2009-01-19.abstract: George J. Mitchell, the former senator from Maine, is said to be a leading candidate to be the Obama administration’s special envoy to the Middle East.

2009-01-20.title: An Uneasy Calm in Gaza
2009-01-20.abstract: Israel accelerated its troop withdrawal as Hamas reasserted control in Gaza and Palestinians sought to recover from the war.

2009-01-20.title: Runoff Aids Some Egyptian Fisheries, Study Says
2009-01-20.abstract: In many regions, farm and sewer runoff poses a deadly threat to marine life, but fisheries off Egypt’s northern coast appear to be thriving from a similar brew.

2009-01-20.title: Tribal Rivalries Persist as Iraqis Seek Local Posts
2009-01-20.abstract: As Sunni groups in Anbar Province jockey for power in the run-up to elections, there is fear that disputes could turn lethal.

2009-01-20.title: On Facebook, Sicilian Mafia Is a Hot Topic
2009-01-20.abstract: Authorities are investigating groups devoted to Mafiosi.

2009-01-20.title: Many Civilian Targets, but One Core Question Among Gazans: Why?
2009-01-20.abstract: Gazans surveying the city on Monday denied that many destroyed civilian institutions, which Israel argued were instruments of Hamas, were legitimate military targets.

2009-01-21.title: Tensions in the Mideast Reverberate in France
2009-01-21.abstract: The conflict between Hamas and Israel has deeply inflamed passions in France, which is home to the largest Jewish and Muslim communities in Western Europe.

2009-01-21.title: Obama Promises the World a Renewed America
2009-01-21.abstract: Some abroad bridled and some were reassured by the recurring foreign policy motif of President Obama’s address.

2009-01-21.title: Debating the Blame for Reducing Much of a Village to Rubble
2009-01-21.abstract: The destruction at Juhr el Dik, a farming village on Gaza’s eastern border, was a hard fact, but how it happened was not, with Israelis and Gazans each offering their own versions of events.

2009-01-21.title: Bombs Kill 5 in Baghdad, but Officials Avoid Harm
2009-01-21.abstract: Violence rose in Baghdad ahead of provincial elections on Jan. 31, with five people killed in two separate bombings.

2009-01-21.title: Russians Strengthen Their Faith and a Tradition With an Icy Water Plunge
2009-01-21.abstract: The annual ritual baptism, which is believed to wash away sins, is enjoying a boisterous revival after being banished to villages during the Soviet era.

2009-01-22.title: Soccer Titan Raises Profile of Tiny Village
2009-01-22.abstract: A little village of just under 3,300 inhabitants has become something of a star in Germany, thanks to the unlikely success of its soccer team.

2009-01-22.title: Seasoned Negotiator to Serve as a Mideast Envoy
2009-01-22.abstract: A former Senate majority leader, George J. Mitchell is viewed as a diplomatic heavyweight who may bring more balance to Washington’s relationships in the region.

2009-01-22.title: On Palestinian Question, Tough Choices for Obama
2009-01-22.abstract: The new administration has to choose between isolating Hamas or supporting a unity government.

2009-01-22.title: China: 2 Held on Way to Dairy Trial
2009-01-22.abstract: Two parents who were traveling to attend criminal proceedings against dairy company executives were detained by the police in two Chinese cities, according to a third parent, Zhao Lianhai.

2009-01-22.title: Israel: High Court Rules for Arab Parties
2009-01-22.abstract: Tthe Israeli Supreme Court ruled Wednesday that the two main Arab political parties, Ra’am-Ta’al and Balad, can run in the national elections on Feb. 10.

2009-01-23.title: Soldier’s Electrocution in Iraq Was Negligent Homicide, Army Concludes
2009-01-23.abstract: An investigation found that negligence by an American contractor that performed electrical work led to the death of a Green Beret electrocuted in a barracks shower.

2009-01-23.title: Hamas to Start Paying Gaza Residents Compensation and Reconstruction Aid
2009-01-23.abstract: Eager to bolster its standing among Gazans after the three-week conflict with Israel, Hamas said it would start to distribute the reconstruction payments on Sunday.

2009-01-23.title: Sri Lanka Presses Rebels, but at a Mounting Cost
2009-01-23.abstract: As the Sri Lankan army makes its deepest encroachment into rebel territory in decades, the cost is growing and the political endgame remains as elusive as ever.

2009-01-23.title: Freed by the U.S., Saudi Becomes a Qaeda Chief
2009-01-23.abstract: The emergence of a former Guantánamo Bay detainee as leader of Al Qaeda’s Yemeni branch underscores the potential complications of closing the site.

2009-01-23.title: Palestinian Rival Says It Is Attacked by Hamas
2009-01-23.abstract: Fatah, Hamas’s political rival, contends its members are harassed by Hamas, as it works to reassert its authority.

2009-01-24.title: In South Africa, a Justice Delayed Is No Longer Denied
2009-01-24.abstract: A gay, white South African judge wrestles with the divisive politics of AIDS at the heart of the pandemic.

2009-01-24.title: As Israeli Bombing Stops, Gazans Get Busy Rebuilding Damaged Tunnels
2009-01-24.abstract: Smuggling tunnels between Egypt and Gaza are the principal livelihood for many people in Rafah, but Israel argues that very soon the tunnels will not matter as much.

2009-01-24.title: Argentina Releases First Picture of Castro in 2 Months
2009-01-24.abstract: The photograph of Fidel Castro was released two days after Argentina’s president visited him in Cuba, and it helped dispel speculation that he was gravely ill.

2009-01-24.title: Desperate Children Flee Zimbabwe, for Lives Just as Desolate
2009-01-24.abstract: Children are rushing across the South African border on their own in a journey made dangerous by thieves and rapists.

2009-01-24.title: Gunmen Kill at Least Six Members of a Family in Iraq
2009-01-24.abstract: The killers’ motives were unclear, but an Iraqi official said a preliminary investigation indicated that some of the women might have been prostitutes.

2009-01-25.title: 2 Ex-Detainees in Qaeda Video
2009-01-25.abstract: Two former Guantánamo Bay detainees now appear to have joined Al Qaeda’s Yemeni branch, which released a video on Friday showing them both and identifying them by their names.

2009-01-25.title: Ruins and Resumption
2009-01-25.abstract: One week after the war between Israel and Hamas stopped — with each side declaring a unilateral cease-fire — Gaza remains in a kind of stupor.

2009-01-25.title: Sri Lanka Captures Rebel Stronghold
2009-01-25.abstract: The government said that its troops had captured the last major town controlled by the ethnic Tamil army.

2009-01-25.title: Congo Presses for Extradition of Warlord
2009-01-25.abstract: There is a growing concern that Rwanda may not hand over Gen. Laurent Nkunda, the captured rebel leader.

2009-01-26.title: Spain and France Clean Up From Storm
2009-01-26.abstract: Powerful gales swept across both countries on Saturday, leaving at least 15 people dead, including 4 children.

2009-01-26.title: 2 Die in Clash in India
2009-01-26.abstract: The police said the militants were killed in a predawn shootout in an eastern suburb of New Delhi, after officers stopped their vehicle to question them.

2009-01-26.title: West African Villagers Stake Their Fortunes on the Future Price of Rice
2009-01-26.abstract: Hoping for a return to last year’s high food prices, the region’s farmers are investing and borrowing heavily to expand production — but rising prices are not guaranteed.

2009-01-26.title: Maliki Pushes for Election Gains, Despite Fears
2009-01-26.abstract: Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki of Iraq is trying to reassure Iraqis that he will respect local interests.

2009-01-26.title: Fighting the Taliban, Losing the Afghans
2009-01-26.abstract: Afghan officials say that when U.S. forces raid villages in their hunt for Taliban fighters, they end up killing civilians and ultimately undermine their cause.

2009-01-27.title: Gaza War Gives Bigger Lift to Israel’s Right Than to Those in Power
2009-01-27.abstract: As Israeli elections draw near, polls indicate that Likud, a right-wing opposition party, has increased its lead since the attacks on Hamas in Gaza.

2009-01-27.title: 9 Alumni of Saudi Program for Ex-Jihadists Are Arrested
2009-01-27.abstract: Graduates of an influential Saudi rehabilitation program for former jihadists have been arrested for rejoining terrorist groups, Saudi officials said Monday.

2009-01-27.title: BBC Assailed for Refusing to Carry Gaza Appeal
2009-01-27.abstract: Citing concern with protecting its impartiality in the Arab-Israeli dispute, the BBC declined to broadcast an appeal by aid agencies for victims of the recent conflict.

2009-01-27.title: Obama Sends Special Envoy to Mideast
2009-01-27.abstract: President Obama sent his special envoy, George J. Mitchell, on an eight-day listening tour that includes Egypt, Israel, Jordan, Saudi Arabia, France and Britain.

2009-01-27.title: Afghan Prison Poses Problem in Overhaul of Detainee Policy
2009-01-27.abstract: While debate over the fate of Guantánamo detainees rages on, a similar problem concerns prisoners held at an air base in Afghanistan.

2009-01-28.title: Group Says It Doesn’t Share Views of Holocaust Denier
2009-01-28.abstract: The St. Pius X Society, a schismatic Catholic society that Pope Benedict XVI recently rehabilitated, distanced itself from the comments of one of its members, who has denied the Holocaust.

2009-01-28.title: Tensions Flare as Russian Soldier Seeks Asylum in Georgia
2009-01-28.abstract: A Russian soldier said he entered Georgia because he was fed up with his military service, but Russian officials say he was abducted and was being forced to discredit the army.

2009-01-28.title: At a Border Crossing, Drivers and Truckloads of Aid for Gaza Go Nowhere
2009-01-28.abstract: This commercial crossing between Egypt and Israel has been turned into a parking lot of stalled humanitarian aid.

2009-01-28.title: Aides Say Obama’s Afghan Aims Elevate War
2009-01-28.abstract: President Obama intends to adopt a tougher line toward President Hamid Karzai and focus more on waging war.

2009-01-28.title: Russian Orthodox Church Elects Outspoken Patriarch
2009-01-28.abstract: Metropolitan Kirill of Smolensk and Kaliningrad was overwhelmingly elected and will be enthroned Sunday as the 16th patriarch of the world’s largest Orthodox church.

2009-01-29.title: Payment Plan for Northern Ireland Reconciliation Provokes Outrage
2009-01-29.abstract: The plan includes a provision for payments to families of all the people killed during 30 years of sectarian violence, including paramilitaries killed by their own bombs.

2009-01-29.title: Canadian Government Survives, Again
2009-01-29.abstract: Canada’s parliamentary turmoil abated, at least temporarily, on Wednesday after an opposition alliance backed down from its efforts to defeat the government.

2009-01-29.title: A Swiss Village Has Peace, Quiet and a Product Endorsed by Marilyn Manson
2009-01-29.abstract: The name Kallnach has become well known thanks to the success of a superpremium absinthe developed by Marilyn Manson.

2009-01-29.title: British Newspaper Says It Caught Lords Offering to Lobby for a Fee
2009-01-29.abstract: All four of the men named by The Sunday Times have denied any wrongdoing, and senior Labor officials have accused the paper of entrapment.

2009-01-29.title: Russia: AWOL Soldier Says He’s Not a Traitor
2009-01-29.abstract: Junior Sgt. Aleksandr Glukhov told a Russian radio station on Wednesday that he had not intended to make a political statement or to betray his country.

2009-01-30.title: New Jungles Prompt a Debate on Rain Forests
2009-01-30.abstract: With abandoned farms reverting to the forest, scientists are debating the urgency of saving the rain forest.

2009-01-30.title: North Korea Scrapping Accords With South Korea
2009-01-30.abstract: North Korea unilaterally declared that it was scrapping agreements it had signed with South Korea to ease military tension on the divided Korean Peninsula.

2009-01-30.title: South African Leader’s Sex Life Stirs Salacious Gossip and Privacy Debate
2009-01-30.abstract: In the wake of a news story about President Kgalema Motlanthe’s multiple alleged paramours, South Africans are weighing a public official’s right to privacy.

2009-01-30.title: Where the Still Flourishing Underground Economy Is the Only Economy
2009-01-30.abstract: Egyptian Rafah has become the focus of an intense effort to stop smuggling through tunnels into Gaza, and the increased security leaves less room for normal life.

2009-01-30.title: Obama Taps a General as the Envoy to Kabul
2009-01-30.abstract: President Obama has picked Lt. Gen. Karl W. Eikenberry, a former top military commander in Afghanistan, to be the next United States ambassador to Kabul.

2009-01-31.title: After Departure, No Leader for U.S. AIDS Program
2009-01-31.abstract: The position is a State Department post with ambassador’s rank that had been held by Dr. Mark R. Dybul, a Bush administration appointee.

2009-01-31.title: British Unions, Angry Over Use of Foreign Workers, Stage Walkouts
2009-01-31.abstract: The disruption underscored rising fears of labor unrest across Europe as job losses across the Continent mounted into the hundreds of thousands with the global financial crisis.

2009-01-31.title: Where Officials See Fraud, Colombia’s Masses See a Folk Hero
2009-01-31.abstract: David Murcia Guzmán has been charged with creating a hydra-headed enterprise that laundered money and enticed thousands of Colombians into a pyramid scheme.

2009-01-31.title: General Says Shoot Dealers in Afghanistan
2009-01-31.abstract: NATO’s senior military commander has proposed that soldiers in Afghanistan shoot drug traffickers without waiting for proof of their involvement with the Taliban insurgency.

2009-01-31.title: Moderate Elected President in Somalia
2009-01-31.abstract: Sheik Sharif Sheik Ahmed, who headed the Islamic courts movement that ruled Somalia’s capital, defeated Maslah Mohamed Siad in the second round of voting.
